title: NASA's Curiosity and Perseverance rovers capture sweeping Mars panoramas (video)
summary: NASA's Curiosity and Perseverance rovers have captured new panoramic views of Mars, showcasing diverse terrains shaped by ancient water and geological processes. The images highlight the planet's dramatic landscapes and evolutionary history.

title: May's full 'Flower Moon' rises tonight: Here's what to expect from the 'micromoon'
summary: May's full 'Flower Moon' will rise tonight, coinciding with the blooming of flowers in the northern hemisphere. This event is noted as a 'micromoon,' where the moon appears smaller due to its distance from Earth.

title: Russia's new homegrown Soyuz 5 rocket aces debut launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its homegrown Soyuz 5 medium-lift rocket on April 30. The debut flight was reported as successful.

title: Artemis 3 has been pushed to late 2027. Can NASA still land astronauts on the moon in 2028?
summary: Artemis 3 has been delayed to late 2027 due to issues with SpaceX's Starship and Blue Origin's Blue Moon lander. This delay raises concerns about NASA's ability to meet the 2028 lunar landing target.

title: US Space Force wants space-based missile interceptors for Golden Dome ready by 2028
summary: The U.S. Space Force is developing space-based missile interceptors under the Golden Dome program, aiming for a demonstration by 2028.

title: Is Venus volcanically active? Big Hawaiian eruption in 2022 could help scientists find out
summary: Evidence suggests Venus may still be volcanically active, and data from a major 2018 eruption in Hawaii could help confirm this. Scientists are analyzing the Hawaiian eruption to better understand volcanic processes on Venus.

title: Astrophotographer captures Pleiades 'Seven Sisters' glowing through ghostly blue veil
summary: An astrophotographer captured a stunning image of the Pleiades star cluster, known as the 'Seven Sisters,' shining through a wispy blue nebula. The photo showcases the blue-white light of the stars reflecting off the ghostly nebula clouds.

title: This SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket launch looks amazing from space in these wild satellite photos
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy rocket launched after an 18-month hiatus, with a satellite capturing stunning images of the event from space.

title: Hubble Spots a Starry Spiral
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the spiral galaxy NGC 3137, located 53 million light-years away in the constellation Antlia. This galaxy provides astronomers with a prime opportunity to study the lifecycle of stars.

title: Cyclone Rains Spur Papua New Guinea Landslides
summary: Heavy rains from Tropical Cyclone Maila caused a deadly landslide in Papua New Guinea's East New Britain region.

title: NASA Invites Media to Ireland Artemis Accords Signing
summary: Ireland is set to sign the Artemis Accords in a ceremony at NASA Headquarters in Washington, hosted by NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and attended by Irish officials. The signing event is scheduled for May 4 at 3 p.m. EDT.

title: Odyssey Team Celebrates on a Global Map of Mars
summary: NASA's 2001 Mars Odyssey orbiter mission team celebrated the spacecraft's 25th launch anniversary on April 15, 2026, with a global map of Mars created using THEMIS imagery.

title: What’s Up: May 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA provides skywatching tips for May 2026, including meteor showers, a lunar conjunction with Venus, and a rare blue moon. The event highlights celestial events visible to the public.

title: Artemis III Moon Rocket Core Stage on the Move
summary: NASA's Artemis III Moon Rocket core stage has been moved to the Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center. The SLS core stage traveled 900 miles from NASA's Michoud Assembly Facility in New Orleans.

title: NASA Welcomes Morocco as 64th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Morocco signed the Artemis Accords on April 29th in Rabat, becoming the 64th signatory nation committed to responsible space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Morocco's participation during the ceremony.

title: NASA Goddard’s Greenbelt Visitor Center Marks 50th Anniversary
summary: NASA Goddard's Greenbelt Visitor Center celebrates its 50th anniversary, having opened in May 1976 to inspire visitors through exhibits and programs on space exploration. The center continues to fulfill its mission of sharing NASA's work with the public.

title: NASA Explores Prioritizing First Response Drones in Crowded Skies
summary: NASA is working to prioritize first response drones in crowded skies as commercial drone use increases. The initiative aims to ensure these critical drones can navigate safely alongside other air traffic.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully conducted the maiden launch of its new Soyuz-5 rocket from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. The debut flight marks a significant milestone for the country's space program.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor discusses their upcoming mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The astronaut has experience in mass casualty events and military service overseas.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the successful Artemis II launch, which carried four astronauts on a lunar flyby mission.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: SpaceX's Falcon Heavy, ULA's Atlas V, and Arianespace's Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Project Kuiper satellites. Six launches are planned from Florida, California, and French Guiana this week.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic clues on Mars, advancing the search for ancient life beyond Earth. The findings were reported by NASASpaceFlight.com.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS), following the CRS NG-24 mission.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on Starship's Block 3 variant at the Cape, with hopes to launch this year. The first flight of Block 3 is approaching as the company continues its development efforts.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the program's timeline and potential impacts on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven missions involving GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches are scheduled for the week of April 20, utilizing four countries and five different launch vehicles. This marks a busy period for space industry activities.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific details or context about space industry news are provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over the past 50 years. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space mission involving a parachute test underwent a critical 'bake-out' process to ensure its reliability in extreme conditions. This procedure helps remove contaminants that could affect the parachute's performance during deployment.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, confirming its readiness for a 2027 launch to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, including an online preparatory session and an in-person event in Paris. The forum aims to gather feedback on topics affecting the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: The article discusses the successful transmission of Celeste's first navigation signal. It highlights the significance of this milestone for the space industry.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has created 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Space Nuclear Execs Cheer the FY27 Budget Proposal
summary: Space nuclear industry executives are optimistic about NASA's FY27 budget proposal, which NASA Administrator Isaacman views as a logical evolution for the agency. The proposal signals strong institutional support beyond just financial backing.

title: House Subcommittee Approves $24.4B NASA Budget
summary: The House Subcommittee has approved a $24.4 billion NASA budget, rejecting the administration's FY27 request. This marks a legislative step to formalize their opposition to the proposed spending plan.

title: European Space Firms Raised €1.4B of Private Capital in 2025
summary: European space firms secured €1.4 billion in private capital in 2025, reflecting growing investor confidence in the sector. This trend highlights the increasing comfort of European private capital markets in supporting space industry startups.

title: BioOrbit Raises £9.8M Seed To Manufacture Cancer Drugs in Orbit
summary: BioOrbit has raised £9.8 million in seed funding to develop cancer drugs in microgravity, aiming to enable self-administration by patients. The company leverages space-based manufacturing to reformulate drugs for improved accessibility and effectiveness.

title: CATALYST Unveils its Sat Product for City Skylines
summary: CATALYST has introduced a new satellite product designed for urban planning and risk assessment. The tool aims to provide construction firms, insurers, and city authorities with insights into pre-existing conditions and potential risks.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire ALL.SPACE for $355M
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring ALL.SPACE for $355 million to expand into high-growth markets like communications and military applications. The deal aims to combine technologies to enhance York's offerings in these sectors.

title: Orbital Data Center Competitive Landscape
summary: The orbital data center market is becoming competitive with major players like SpaceX, Blue Origin, and Sophia Space. Additional contributors include satellite manufacturers such as Apex and K2 Space.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit compute capabilities by running a large language model on Liquid AI's behalf to analyze an Earth observation image directly in space. The mission, Clustergate-2, launched aboard Momentus' Vigoride 7 satellite in March.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, according to space executives. Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon emphasized that the goal is for it to be indistinguishable from commercial alternatives.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticized former President Trump's decision to cut the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it harmful to science and American innovation. The statement was made by Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the policy change.

title: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani Talks Network Resiliency at Modern Day Marine
summary: Lite Coms USG Sales Director George Cerasani discussed the company's tactical, multi-orbit connectivity solutions at Modern Day Marine. The focus was on network resiliency in the space industry.

title: Skynopy Signs Frequency Management Partnership with CNES
summary: Skynopy has partnered with CNES to optimize frequency allocation for its ground stations in France. The collaboration aims to enhance frequency management for satellite operations.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Terminal Developer All.Space in $355M Deal
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring terminal developer All.Space in a $355 million deal to create a complete communications ecosystem. The acquisition aims to enhance York's capabilities in satellite communications.

title: FCC Updates EPFD Framework that Covers GEO/NGSO Spectrum Sharing
summary: The FCC adopted a new framework for Equivalent Power Flux Density (EPFD) to govern spectrum-sharing between GEO and NGSO systems. This update addresses longstanding concerns about interference and efficient use of satellite spectrum.

title: EarthDaily CEO Don Osborne Previews Upcoming Launch in Thursday Morning Conversation
summary: EarthDaily Analytics is preparing for a critical launch of six satellites this quarter, marking a transition to commercial service. CEO Don Osborne discussed the upcoming launch in a recent conversation.

title: Department of Air Force Plans Initiative to Hire AI Talent
summary: The U.S. Department of the Air Force (DAF) is launching an initiative to recruit top AI talent, which will impact the government's space strategy.

title: Canada Cancels WildFireSat Constellation Contract With Spire
summary: Canada has canceled its contract with Spire Global to build the WildFireSat wildfire-monitoring satellite constellation, but the government still plans to proceed with the space-based wildfire monitoring program.

title: SpaceX Launches Final ViaSat-3 Satellite, Completing Trio of GEO Satellites
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the final ViaSat-3 satellite, completing Viasat's trio of high-capacity Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites. The mission used a Falcon Heavy rocket from Kennedy Space Center.

title: Orbcomm Completes $460M Refinancing to Fund Growth
summary: Orbcomm has completed a $460 million refinancing to support growth initiatives, large-scale customer deployments, and platform expansion. The refinancing was announced on Wednesday.

title: UPCOMING: Designing Secure Satellite Systems with FIPS 140-3, CNSA 2.0, and PQC
summary: Designing secure satellite systems is becoming increasingly challenging due to evolving security requirements like CNSA 2.0 and FIPS 140-3, especially for long-term deployments with limited updates.
